base_final_evaluation_protocol.immutability_policy: This addendum does not modify, replace, or supersede the frozen final primary-test evaluation protocol.

boundary_history_context.meaning: These are the 12 timestamps immediately preceding the first primary-test target start. They are used only as observed causal input history for the earliest test windows and never as evaluation targets.

calibration_protection_policy.allowed_exception: Only timestamps [41680, 41692) may be read as causal history inputs for the first 12 frozen primary-test windows. No value from this interval may be used as a forecast target, metric target, loss target, model selection signal, tuning signal, or normalization source.

frozen_window_contract.history_definition: For target start t, history is [t-12, t-1] and forecast targets are [t, t+11].

nonnegotiable_invariance.checkpoint_selection: Use only pre-saved checkpoints selected earlier by the earliest minimum fixed-composite selection raw-speed MAE.
nonnegotiable_invariance.controlled_dropout_conditions: Use the frozen clean condition and all nine frozen primary-test controlled-dropout masks unchanged.
nonnegotiable_invariance.normalization: Use only frozen train-partition sensor means and standard deviations.

publication_language_constraint: Calibration was not used for checkpoint selection, hyperparameter tuning, threshold fitting, conformal fitting, or evaluation. The only calibration-index overlap was the mandatory 12-timestamp causal input-history context directly preceding the primary-test interval.
purpose: Clarify the unavoidable causal-history boundary context required to evaluate the earliest frozen primary-test windows under the pre-registered 12-step history contract.
